data.body:
PASS-1 G6 review (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) of the kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering FOLLOW-ON plan ("T12 gap the gate found on itself"). Findings are coaching, non-blocking; grounded in shipped code.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE / NIH: re-ground the plan's central premise against the shipped code before building — the "disable-the-gate config (fail-open)" the plan proposes to add (Scope item 1 / AC1) ALREADY EXISTS, so as written the approach rebuilds an existing mechanism rather than solving the residual gap.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "Why" + "Scope" item 1 + Acceptance Criteria line 1
  - evidence:
    - `src/rebar/config.py:260` — `require_plan_review_for_claim: bool = False` (DEFAULT OFF); inline comment :258-259 reads "Default off ⇒ `claim` keeps today's behavior; turning it off is the rollback (an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed)."
    - `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:661-662` — `_plan_review_precheck` returns `None` (allow, claim unchanged) when the flag is off; :652-660 fails OPEN on unreadable config with a warning. This is exactly the project-level, fail-open disable switch Scope item 1 / AC1 propose.
    - `rebar.toml:14-21` — documents the flag and states "Turning this off is the rollback."
    - The parent epic's own Success Criterion #4 already specifies this design ("a config PREFERENCE that turns the plan-review requirement ON/OFF … DEFAULTS OFF … 'rollback' is simply turning the preference off … no separate rollback mechanism is needed").
  - suggested_fix: Rewrite "Why"/"Scope" to acknowledge `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default False) IS the disable config and IS the behavioral rollback; reframe AC1 from "A config flag disables the gate … covered by a test" to "confirm/document the existing default-off preference satisfies this (and fill any genuine doc/test gap)."

- [ ] AC1's "covered by a test" clause appears already met — verify before treating it as new work, so the plan does not re-specify existing coverage.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan AC line 1 ("… fail-open … when off; covered by a test")
  - evidence: `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py:69` `test_gate_off_by_default_claims_without_attestation` asserts the gate-off path leaves `claim` at today's behavior; the suite docstring (:9) and the on/off toggle are also covered. Point AC1 at the existing test or name the precise missing assertion.

- [ ] Reconcile Scope item 2's old/new coexistence clause ("older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events") with the SHIPPED forward-compat handling, which already implements it — this part is fitness-for-purpose duplication, not a gap.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "Scope" item 2 + AC line 2 (coexistence clause)
  - evidence: `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44` — `REVIEW_RESULT` is in `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`, so it is reducer-IGNORED and fsck-recognized; the same preserve-and-ignore machinery the plan asks to "cross-check with the TAG_DELTA/event-rollout note" is documented at :31-37. The parent epic's db7b note already records "REVIEW_RESULT + attestation events follow rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout." State what (if anything) is still missing, or fold this into the verification.

- [ ] STATE THE POSITIVE RATIONALE tied to the actual code state, and re-target the plan onto its genuine residual. The plan's rationale ("a gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure") rests on a premise contradicted by the shipped default-off, opt-in gate. The genuinely-unaddressed slice the verifier signal points at is NARROWER: deploy-ordering for the signature/REVIEW_RESULT sidecar scheme vs the claim-path check, and rollback of the PERSISTED SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT event artifacts after a binary downgrade (turning the preference off reverts gate BEHAVIOR, not the persisted event-log artifacts). If after re-grounding a real gap survives there, state why THAT specific gap warrants the ticket; if none survives, the positive rationale is "verify + document," which should be stated explicitly so an implementer does not build a redundant disable mechanism.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan "Why" section (whole) + Scope item 3 (deploy ordering)

APPROACH-SELECTION NOTE (G6.3): I generated the structurally-different alternative the plan missed — treat this as a VERIFICATION / DOC-AUDIT task against the already-shipped default-off preference + preserved-and-ignored rollout, scoped to (a) a project-facing back-out RUNBOOK for the signature scheme and (b) deploy-ordering for the SIGNATURE head-state event vs an old reducer, rather than a build task that adds a second disable mechanism. On codebase-alignment (the off-switch + event coexistence already exist), blast-radius (zero new config vs. a redundant second kill-switch), simplicity, and robustness (avoids two competing disable concepts), this alternative is clearly superior to "build a new disable-config + rollback path." Coaching the planner to adopt it.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan satisfies once re-scoped):
- The advisory verifier's confidence-.71/critical signal correctly identifies that rollout/rollback DOCUMENTATION for the signing scheme is worth surfacing; a project-facing back-out runbook + deploy-ordering writeup is a defensible, fit-for-purpose doc deliverable even though the disable-config and event-coexistence mechanisms already ship.
- The plan correctly names the right artifacts to cross-check (the TAG_DELTA/event-rollout note, the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar) — the data-flow it points at is real and resolves to actual source.
